root_text.sn22.153:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn22.153:1.3: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn22.153:1.5: “rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn22.153:1.6: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn22.153:1.10: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa, evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn22.153:1.11: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn22.153:1.15: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn22.153:1.17: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn22.153:1.18: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn22.153:1.25: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn22.153:1.27: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn22.153:1.28: ‘no cassaṁ, no ca me siyā, nābhavissa, na me bhavissatī’”ti?

translation_text.sn22.153:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn22.153:1.3: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine’?”
translation_text.sn22.153:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn22.153:1.5: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn22.153:1.6: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.sn22.153:1.10: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, the view arises:
translation_text.sn22.153:1.11: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.sn22.153:1.15: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn22.153:1.17: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would the view arise:
translation_text.sn22.153:1.18: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine’?”
translation_text.sn22.153:1.25: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn22.153:1.27: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would the view arise:
translation_text.sn22.153:1.28: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine’?”
translation_text.sn22.153:1.31: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’”
